purpose: Top-of-IDE horizontal tab bar listing all open workspace folders with a '+' add button. Each tab shows folder icon + name + close (×). On mount, runs a one-shot recovery effect (recoveryRanRef): for each persisted workspace, validates its dashboard exists in dashboardList; if missing, calls removeWorkspaceDashboard(wsId) + createWorkspaceDashboard(wsId) + saveDashboardProject + dispatches IDE_LINK_WORKSPACE_DASHBOARD. handleAddWorkspace opens native picker, generates wsId=String(Date.now()), dispatches IDE_OPEN_WORKSPACE, then creates the dashboard, links it via IDE_LINK_WORKSPACE_DASHBOARD, and SWITCH_DASHBOARD. handleSwitchWorkspace dispatches IDE_SWITCH_WORKSPACE and SWITCH_DASHBOARD to the workspace's dashboard so chat context follows the active folder. handleCloseWorkspace deletes the dashboard from disk via window.electronAPI.deleteDashboard, dispatches REMOVE_DASHBOARD, removes localStorage mapping, and switches to a fallback dashboard if the closed one was current.
gotchas: Mount-time recovery effect uses recoveryRanRef to ensure it runs ONCE per mount — if it fails partway, retrying requires a remount.

Recovery dep array is [dashboardList, ideWorkspaces] — but recoveryRanRef gates execution to first run. If dashboardList loads after ideWorkspaces (race), the effect re-runs with correct data and the ref guard prevents re-execution. Keep the ref guard.

createWorkspaceDashboard call duplicated in IDEWelcome.jsx (cold-start path) AND here (warm path) — but IDEWelcome does NOT dispatch IDE_LINK_WORKSPACE_DASHBOARD, leading to a subtle desync where ws.dashboardId is null until the next recovery run.
handleCloseWorkspace switches dashboard FIRST, then deletes — order matters because deleting current dashboard would leave UI in a broken state.
deleteDashboard IPC is awaited but errors are only logged — user gets no feedback if disk delete fails.
Workspace ID generation via String(Date.now()) — same collision risk as IDEWelcome (rare).
Comment in source: 'Sidebar.jsx only handles display, sorting, and orphan cleanup — never creation.' — i.e., dashboard creation is owned by THIS file (and IDEWelcome) exclusively.
patterns: One-shot mount-time recovery effect via useRef boolean guard — recreates missing dashboards after app restart.
Workspace↔Dashboard linking has THREE storage layers: (1) ideWorkspaceManager localStorage map, (2) ws.dashboardId in React state, (3) saveDashboardProject mapping. All three must agree.
handleCloseWorkspace is a 4-step ordered cascade: get mapping → switch away if current → close in state → delete on disk → REMOVE_DASHBOARD action.
conventions: CSS class 'workspace-tabs' container, 'workspace-tab' base + ' active' modifier, 'workspace-tab-close' for ×.
